title: Real-world effectiveness of SGLT2 inhibitors and kidney outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes and CKD
abstract: BACKGROUND: Real-world data on SGLT2 inhibitors in patients with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease (CKD) are limited. We conducted a population-based retrospective cohort study using administrative claims data. METHODS: We identified new users of SGLT2 inhibitors compared with new users of GLP-1 receptor agonists. The primary outcome was progression to end-stage kidney disease (ESKD). RESULTS: Among 35,470 patients, SGLT2 inhibitor use was associated with a significantly lower risk of ESKD (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.54-0.86) compared with GLP-1 agonist use, after propensity score weighting. LIMITATIONS: Residual confounding from unmeasured variables cannot be excluded in this observational design.
journal: Journal of the American Society of Nephrology
